steps.title: Identify the opening modifier
steps.content: The phrase Hoping to help her students and their families outside the classroom is an introductory participial phrase. It modifies Huerta, the subject of the main clause.

steps.title: Separate modifier and clause
steps.content: Choice A turns the introductory phrase into a fragment. Choice B uses a semicolon, which requires an independent clause on both sides. Choice D omits the punctuation required after the introductory modifier. A comma correctly separates the modifier from the main clause. The answer is C.
